DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
example extensions link metadata rich-text status usecase
component pattern style theme token
accessibility anatomy api best-practice design-specifications guideline interaction principle purpose scale state variant

DSDS Use Case Definitions

Definitions for documenting when to use and when not to use a design system artifact or option. Use cases provide scenario-driven guidance that complements best practices by framing decisions in terms of concrete situations rather than abstract rules. The useCase and useCases defs defined here carry no guideline `type` discriminator — they are the raw data model used for nesting inside variant values, state entries, and as the content of the purpose guideline type.

Source: common/usecase.schema.json

2 definitions in this file:

  • useCase
  • useCases

useCase

A single use case scenario describing a situation in which an artifact or option is or is not appropriate.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
descriptionrichTextrequiredA description of the scenario. Should be concrete and situation-driven — describe the user's situation, not the artifact's features.
alternativeobjectoptionalThe recommended alternative artifact for this scenario. Typically provided for whenNotToUse entries to direct the reader toward a more appropriate component, pattern, or token.
examplesexample[]optionalOptional examples illustrating the use case scenario.

References: richText, example

useCases

Scenario-driven guidance describing when an artifact or option is and is not the right choice. Contains whenToUse and whenNotToUse arrays of use case entries. This def carries no guideline `type` discriminator — it is the raw data model for nesting inside other structures (variant values, state entries) and as the content payload of the purpose guideline type.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
whenToUseuseCase[]optionalScenarios in which this artifact or option is the appropriate choice. Each entry should be scenario-driven — describe the user's situation, not the artifact's features. For example, 'When the user needs to trigger an action such as submitting a form' rather than 'When you need a clickable element'.
whenNotToUseuseCase[]optionalScenarios in which this artifact or option should not be used. Each entry SHOULD include an `alternative` pointing to a more appropriate artifact or pattern, along with a rationale explaining why the alternative is better.

References: useCase
